questions.question: Hi Soumyadeep, Soumyadeep,  tell me about your experience as an HHA – what aspects did you enjoy most, and what were some of the biggest learning curves?
Soumyadeep,  tell me about a time a client was difficult or uncooperative. How did you handle that situation?
Soumyadeep, imagine a client refuses their medication; how would you approach that situation to ensure their safety and well-being?
Soumyadeep, your CPR certification is valuable.  Describe a time you used a skill learned in your training to help a client.
Soumyadeep, you mentioned wanting 33 hours a week.  How would you manage stress and maintain your energy levels consistently?

responses.response: I have been involved in caregiving for a long time. Uh so I had a grandmother who actually passed because I could not care for her. So this motivated me to pursue this career so that I could provide a better caregiving experience for a lot of different uh adults around me.

Yeah. So I had a client who was 82 years of age who was very, um, like uncooperative. I mean, she could not understand what I was saying. She was suffering from like a like a dementia as well. Um, like she refused to take her medicine sometimes. But it was a good experience that I was able to help her and uh get an understanding of what is important of uh getting her to take her medicine and stuff.

So there was a guy that was um like a like a drowning when I was went to beach uh but nobody was watching him. So I went inside and dived deep into sea and took him out and then gave him a CPR which actually eventually saved his life. And so I am really proud of uh this certification and hope to use it uh in my day-to-day work.

Uh yes uh I work 30 hours a week. So uh could not be like I think it could not be very uh difficult task for me. I have been doing for quite some time. So I actually take rest and naps in between uh my work as well. Uh properly uh like I also take supplement. My diet is also good enough. And also I go to the gym daily. And so uh like we are training uh help me and I think this all are training uh me
